DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (RFP). RFP NOS. 27 - 752 ARE OVERBROAD AND SEEK INFORMATION INTO PARTY ASSETS THAT ARE NOT FUNDAMENTAL TO THE CASE. (CCP 2031.030.) RFP NO. 72 AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 3, 4, 6 - 9 RELATE TO MATTERS OF PUBLIC RECORD THAT ARE EQUALLY AVAILABLE TO THE PROPOUNDING PARTY AND SEEK INFORMATION INTO PARTY ASSETS THAT ARE NOT FUNDAMENTAL TO THE CASE. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 17 - 19, 29 - 31 PERTAIN TO SKYLAKE DEVELOPMENT, LLC AND SKYLAKE REAL ESTATE, INC., WHO ARE BOTH PARTIES TO THE COMPLAINT AND CROSS-COMPLAINT, AND MR. WALSH SHALL PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AS TO THE INFORMATION REGARDING THESE ENTITIES AND HIS PARTICIPATION IN THESE ENTITIES. SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 16, 20, 24, 28 ARE TOO VAGUE AND TENUOUS TO SUPPORT AN INTRUSION INTO THE FINANCIAL AFFAIRS OF NON-PARTIES. (BRITT V. SUP.CT. (1978) 20 CAL.3D 844,855.) SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 10, 12, 35, 39, 40, 41, 44, 46 ARE SUFFICIENTLY SPECIFIC AND ARE ROUTINE IN NATURE. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER, FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED. Off Calendar Feb-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL E. HANNA OF MORRIS POLICH & PURDY LLP, TEL. NO. 984-8521 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;
ATTY. QUEENA HO APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT WILLIAM PORTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIAM PORTER's Motion To Compel Further Responses From cross-defendant Walsh To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2)Special Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions IS Granted as to Special Interrogatories nos. 10, 12, 17 - 19, 29 - 31, 35, 39, 40, 41 44 and 46. Denied as to all other Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production (RFP). RFP nos. 27 - 752 are overbroad and seek information into party assets that are not fundamental to the case. (CCP 2031.030.) RFP no. 72 and Special Interrogatories nos. 3, 4, 6 - 9 relate to matters of public record that are equally available to the propounding party and seek information into party assets that are not fundamental to the case. Special Interrogatories nos. 17 - 19, 29 - 31 pertain to Skylake Development, LLC and Skylake Real Estate, Inc., who are both parties to the Complaint and Cross-Complaint, and Mr. Walsh shall provide further responses as to the information regarding these entities and his participation in these entities. Special Interrogatories nos. 16, 20, 24, 28 are too vague and tenuous to support an intrusion into the financial affairs of non-parties. (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844,855.) Special Interrogatories nos. 10, 12, 35, 39, 40, 41, 44, 46 are sufficiently specific and are routine in nature. Within 10 days of this order, further responses shall be served. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG
